lineage: Heretics Not to Be Allowed to Argue Out of the Scriptures. The Scriptures, in Fact, Do Not Belong to Them.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: Heretics Not to Be Allowed to Argue Out of the Scriptures. The Scriptures, in Fact, Do Not Belong to Them.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.text: We are therefore come to (the gist of) our position; for at this point we were aiming, and for this we were preparing in the preamble of our address (which we have just completed),—so that we may now join issue on the contention to which our adversaries challenge us.
They put forward the Scriptures, and by this insolence of theirs they at once influence some.
In the encounter itself, however, they weary the strong, they catch the weak, and dismiss waverers with a doubt.
Accordingly, we oppose to them this step above all others, of not admitting them to any discussion of the Scriptures.

If in these lie their resources, before they can use them, it ought to be clearly seen to whom belongs the possession of the Scriptures, that none may be admitted to the use thereof who has no title at all to the privilege.
